scores.critique: TECHNICALLY THE STRONGEST. Every defect-mechanism claim was verified against code and is correct. F1=(a) gate retry on is_idempotent() directly severs the confirmed double-spawn mechanism (daemon_client.rs:191-201 re-sends the cloned CommandStartCombed on transport timeout). This is the SMALLEST change that closes the actual regression, and research-gaps.json explicitly says researchers under-weighted exactly this option ('the cheapest correct fix may actually be (b) [sic, (a)]... DISABLE the blind retry for non-idempotent mutating RPCs'). It correctly identifies the EXHAUSTIVE match requirement so a future variant is force-classified (the IpcRequest enum at protocol.rs:199-288 confirms a closed variant set, so this compiles cleanly). Crucially, it does NOT introduce the false-collapse race that the daemon-side in-flight fingerprint (chosen by the other two plans) carries: R2 open_question warns a fingerprint 'risks collapsing two legitimately-distinct rapid identical runs' — two distinct `npm test` invocations within the TTL window would be silently deduped into one job_id, a NEW correctness bug. Minimal-risk AVOIDS that by deferring the dedup guard and relying on the honest remedy text + recovery contract instead. The remedy-text fix is verified necessary: transport_unavailable_error (tools.rs:1797-1808) does route through McpError::internal_error (-32603) AND says 'retry the tool' verbatim — exactly the orchestrator-confirmed footgun. F2 reuses the existing complete/wait_exhausted contract (run_and_watch_completion at tools.rs:1768) as a strict superset — verified the existing payload at tools.rs:726-736 supports additive degraded/recover_hint with zero schema-gate breakage (R3 confirms no schema test inspects run_and_watch result fields). F3 wall-clock deadline is correct and honestly bounds residual to 'wait_ms + one slice + RTTs'. F4/F5/F6 all match research exactly (cancel_tx clone before move at command.rs:620 avoids the PTY Arc<Mutex> lock-across-await footgun R2 flags; cached immortal bucket honors source.rs:12-17; argv[0..2] redacted reconciles R-06; into_parts tag:None fix at tools.rs:2277 confirmed). DOCKED 1pt each: (correctness) the honest-remedy residual still leaves a >5s-spawning command timing out client-side and the agent must manually recover via command_status — the ghost job still EXISTS, it is just non-double-spawned and recoverable; this is an acceptable engineering tradeoff but not a full close of TC-1a's underlying client-timeout mechanism (the plan is HONEST about this in its F1 rationale, which is why it scores high). (completeness) defers F1-b idempotency key — defensible given retry removal, but leaves the residual manual-retry double-spawn path open (mitigated, not eliminated). The is_idempotent classification of PtyCommandWriteStdin as non-idempotent is conservative-correct (writing stdin twice is not safe to auto-retry). Phasing is genuinely PR-sized and each phase is independently green; TC-3 isolated last is correct sequencing for the atomic-count-anchor risk.

STRONG and the most rigorously-reasoned on the contract axiom, but introduces ONE genuine new-correctness risk that minimal-risk avoids. F1 correctly adopts (a) classify-and-don't-retry as PRIMARY — same verified-correct core as minimal-risk — but then ADDS a daemon-side in-flight fingerprint = hash(argv+cwd+tag) with a 3s TTL as 'defense-in-depth' (Phase 1, command.rs:~459). THIS IS THE FATAL NUANCE: a fingerprint-collapse guard that returns the SAME job_id for a second identical CommandStartCombed within 3s will SILENTLY DEDUPE two legitimately-distinct rapid identical runs (e.g. an agent deliberately running `git status` twice in quick succession, or two concurrent agents). R2 open_question flagged this exact risk ('risks collapsing two legitimately-distinct rapid identical runs'). The plan does not specify how a caller DISTINGUISHES a deduped-return from a fresh spawn, nor how the second caller learns its run was collapsed — so it can introduce a worse trust violation (caller thinks it started a job; it got someone else's) than the one it fixes. Once the blind retry is GONE (which this plan also does), the fingerprint guard is defending against only the manual-LLM-retry path, which the honest remedy text already discourages — so the added race is poorly justified. This costs correctness (-2) and risk (-3). It also slightly violates scope discipline (-3 vs minimal-risk's -1): the fingerprint touches command.rs spawn-path state (a HashMap with TTL removal on EVERY completion path — exit, cancel, spawn-failure — which the plan's own risk note admits 'must be removed on EVERY completion path or it leaks/blocks a legitimate re-run', a real new failure mode). Everything ELSE is excellent and matches verified code: F2 superset-payload contract is the best-articulated of the three (explicitly a strict superset so no existing reader breaks); F3 wall-clock deadline + lowering MAX_WAIT_SLICE_MS 1000->500 is correct BUT the plan's own risk note flags it 'doubles the RPC count per second; verify it does not regress the load gate (load_noise_backpressure)' — a real CI risk it must explicitly run, costing nothing extra since it's acknowledged. F4 command_stop, F5 self_check, F6 argv all match research precisely. Phasing (6 phases, TC-1a/TC-2 split from the in-flight dedup is actually CLEANER than minimal-risk for the fingerprint, but the fingerprint shouldn't be there). The numeric -32603 deferral is correctly flagged (verified: transport_unavailable_error stays -32603). Net: more complete than minimal-risk on TC-2 closure, but buys it with a self-inflicted dedup race.

HIGHEST AMBITION, HIGHEST RISK, and it does the one thing research-gaps.json explicitly warned against. F1 = (a) no-retry + (b) idempotency_key on RequestEnvelope + (c) pre-spawn ack with Starting state — ALL THREE layered. This is exactly the 'jumped to the heavier design' that R2/research-gaps flag as under-weighting option (a). FATAL FLAW 1: Phase 3 (pre-spawn ack) is the single riskiest change in any plan. R2 finding Q1b confirms it 'changes the spawn-failure contract': today ProcessProbe::spawn failure returns CommandError::Spawn synchronously with NO job registered (command.rs:551-560); with a pre-spawn ack, a spawn FAILURE must instead flip an already-acked Starting job to Failed and synthesize a command_failed via bucket/audit, AND run_and_watch/command_status must learn the new Starting liveness at the wire boundary (R2 open_question: how does liveness.rs map Starting?). This is a protocol-and-lifecycle redesign that the plan compresses into a 4-file phase ('Starting+ack; spawn-fail->Failed+synthetic') with near-zero detail. It is NOT independently shippable without F1-a+b already landed, and stacking ack ON TOP of both no-retry AND idempotency-key is redundant machinery: once retry is gone and the envelope has an idempotency key, the ack adds orphan-window recovery that the degraded-result contract (F2) already provides. FATAL FLAW 2: SCOPE EXPLOSION — 8 code phases (vs 5-6) with two of them (P2 idempotency-key threading 6 files, P3 pre-spawn ack 4 files) being net-new protocol/lifecycle surface the defects do not require. This violates the 'minimal correct change' principle and the campaign's PR-sized-phase constraint in spirit. FATAL FLAW 3: DETAIL POVERTY — the plan is extremely terse (single-clause changes, no per-fork rationale beyond 6-12 words). 'F4 command_stop forced-kill via dormant CommandSignal; clone cancel_tx before move(command.rs:620); audit-before-kill' is directionally CORRECT and matches R2, but a judge cannot verify the atomic-anchor handling (5 lists + 3 counts) is understood — P7 lists 'counts 37->38; fixtures 38/34' which IS the right math, so the author knows it, but the compression makes review-confidence low. WHAT IT GETS RIGHT (why not lower): the root-cause diagnosis is accurate, F2 degraded-keeps-job_id is correct, F3 combined CommandWaitNext RPC is a legitimate (if heavier) TC-6 option that genuinely co-locates one error site, F5/F6 match research, the invariants list is complete and correct (additive serde(default), mcp/src spawn-free, both OS gates, atomic anchors, live-daemon-untouched). The no-fake-success invariant ('P3->Failed, P6 failures>0, P4 degraded keeps job_id') shows correct intent. But correctness is undercut by shipping the highest-blast-radius option (pre-spawn ack) that R2 specifically warns 'still double-spawns under the blind retry unless paired with the dedup guard' — meaning the author is building three overlapping fixes where one (a) suffices, and the most dangerous one (c) is under-specified.

best_ideas_to_graft: From minimal-risk and trust-contract-first: F1=(a) gate the daemon_client.rs:191 retry on request.is_idempotent() with an EXHAUSTIVE match on IpcRequest, returning false for CommandStartCombed/PtyCommandStart/PtyCommandWriteStdin/Registry{Upsert,Activate,Deactivate,ImportPack}/FileWatch{Start,Stop}/Shutdown. This is the verified-minimal close of the double-spawn mechanism and research-gaps.json explicitly says it was under-weighted. Adopt this as the spine of Phase 1 in any final plan.

From minimal-risk: fix transport_unavailable_error (tools.rs:1804-1805) to DROP 'retry the tool' for the mutating case and say 'the command MAY have started; call command_status/runtime_state to check before re-running'. Verified: it currently routes through McpError::internal_error (-32603) with that exact harmful remedy. Required regardless of which F1 combination is chosen.

From trust-contract-first and minimal-risk: F2 degraded result as a STRICT SUPERSET of the existing run_and_watch payload (reuse complete/wait_exhausted from run_and_watch_completion, add only degraded:true + recover_hint + cursor). trust-contract-first's 'strict superset so no existing reader breaks' framing is the cleanest articulation; verified no schema test gates run_and_watch result fields (R3).

From all three: F3 wall-clock Instant deadline replacing the fixed slice count, with per-iteration BucketWait timeout_ms = min(MAX_WAIT_SLICE_MS, remaining). Co-design F2+F3 in ONE wait-loop rewrite (tools.rs:645-709) — verified same loop body, research-gaps says they MUST be co-designed. Prefer minimal-risk's deadline-only approach over trust-contract-first's additional MAX_WAIT_SLICE_MS 1000->500 unless the load gate (load_noise_backpressure) is explicitly re-run, since halving the slice doubles RPC rate.

From minimal-risk (F4): clone the oneshot cancel_tx BEFORE the probe moves into drive_to_exit (command.rs:620) and store it in JobBinding — strictly smaller and safer than Arc<Mutex<Option<ProcessProbe>>>, avoiding the PTY lock-across-await footgun R2 documents. Gate command_stop on the dormant PolicyAction::CommandSignal with audit-before-kill (improves on PTY's ungated stop).

From minimal-risk (F5): host the spawn round-trip in the IPC handle_self_check (the surface that lies to live clients), make it async, reuse ONE cached immortal self-check bucket, and profile-gate to skip-or-assert-deny (never false-RED under read_only_observer). Add a NEGATIVE test that forces the round-trip to fail and asserts failures>0 — proves it is no longer a hardcoded green.

From minimal-risk and trust-contract-first (F6): fix the into_parts tag:None latent fake-success path (tools.rs:2277) AND put crates/cli/src/render.rs explicitly IN SCOPE so the new tag/argv_head fields are not silently dropped at the CLI consumer. Use serde(default, skip_serializing_if) additive ProbeListEntry fields matching the frames_suppressed/liveness precedent.

From root-cause-maximalist's invariants: the explicit no-fake-success ledger ('degraded keeps job_id, self_check failures>0 on real breakage') is a good acceptance-gate framing to bake into every phase's verification section.
fatal_flaws: root-cause-maximalist Phase 3 (pre-spawn ack with Starting state): under-specified protocol+lifecycle redesign that changes the spawn-failure contract (command.rs:551-560 must flip an already-acked job to Failed + synthesize command_failed) and exposes a new Starting liveness at the wire boundary that run_and_watch/command_status/liveness.rs must learn. R2 explicitly warns this 'still double-spawns under the blind retry unless paired with the dedup guard' — so layering it on top of no-retry AND an idempotency key is redundant, highest-blast-radius machinery for a defect that option (a) alone closes. Compressed into a 4-file phase with no liveness-mapping detail.

trust-contract-first AND root-cause-maximalist daemon-side in-flight fingerprint = hash(argv+cwd+tag): introduces a NEW correctness bug — silently collapsing two legitimately-distinct rapid identical runs (or two concurrent agents) into one shared job_id, with no specified mechanism for the second caller to learn its run was deduped. R2 open_question flagged exactly this collapse risk. Once the blind retry is removed (which both plans also do), this guard only defends the manual-retry path that the honest remedy text already discourages, so the added race is poorly justified. trust-contract-first compounds it with TTL-removal-on-every-completion-path as a new leak/block failure mode (its own risk note admits this).

root-cause-maximalist scope/PR-size: 8 code phases with two net-new protocol/lifecycle surfaces (idempotency-key envelope threading + pre-spawn ack) that the six defects do not require — violates the minimal-correct-change principle and the campaign's independently-green-PR-sized-phase constraint. Building three overlapping TC-1a fixes where (a) suffices.

All three plans share a RESIDUAL (not fatal, but un-closed by the chosen F1): with only retry-gating, a >5s-spawning command STILL times out client-side and the client STILL loses the job_id (the ghost job exists; it is just non-double-spawned and recoverable via the degraded result + named runtime_state rows). Minimal-risk is HONEST about this and tracks the durable idempotency-key/ack fixes as deferred BACKLOG/RISK rows; the other two attempt to close it but introduce the dedup race instead. None FULLY closes the underlying client-timeout-aborts-live-spawn mechanism without either the dedup race or the ack redesign — the correct posture is minimal-risk's: stop the harm (double-spawn), make the residual recoverable+honest, and defer the durable protocol fix with tracking.

root-cause-maximalist detail poverty: single-clause change descriptions make it impossible to verify the author has correctly handled the atomic anchor set (5 name lists + 3 count assertions + fixture map + system_discover fixture + minimal_tool_args) for command_stop and the 6+ sync surfaces for a new IPC method — the highest 'easy to half-fix => CI red' trap (R3). The math shown ('37->38; fixtures 38/34') is right, but the compression leaves no reviewable evidence the full chain is understood.

scores.critique: Strongest plan for a CI-gates pass-probability judgment. Verified against code, every load-bearing anchor it names is exact: catalogue_lists_thirty_seven_live_tools (tools.rs:3232, ordered vec), tool_router_exposes_all_live_tools (3292, sorted vec), mcp_stdio + mcp_live_daemon sorted lists, system_discover.v1.json tools[], the assert_eq!(checked, 36) + its literal comment math (daemon_unavailable_envelope.rs:214-217), the fixture-map counts block (live_tools 37->38, covered_live 33->34 -- I confirmed the file shows 37/33/4-missing), minimal_tool_args (correctly notes command_stop with job_id-only can reuse the existing command_status|pty_command_stop arm), and the full IPC 6+ surface chain incl. re-exports and the system_discover_methods_match_dispatch parity test. F1=(a) is verified feasible: daemon_client.rs:189-198 has `request` in scope (clone-then-retry), so a request.is_idempotent() guard is exactly the trivial pure-data edit claimed, guard-safe (no spawn/fs/socket). It correctly identifies that transport_unavailable_error() is a SHARED no-arg helper whose remedy literally says 'retry the tool' (verified tools.rs:1804-1805) and must be split mutating/non-mutating -- a subtlety Plan 3 misses. Both OS gates per phase, TESTING.md 3.4 integration tests for command_stop, source-status labels on every test, zero-deps invariant, BACKLOG/RISK_REGISTER add-only (R-07/08/09), .planning open-work multi-file shape, docs-only Phase 0 correctly noting npm-binary-build.yml has no paths filter so the full 8 required contexts still run. F2+F3 correctly co-located in one wait-loop rewrite (shared tools.rs:655-709). The one genuine deduction: F1=(a) alone leaves TC-2 with NO actual dedup guard -- it reframes 'no idempotency guard anywhere' as 'auto-retry path removed' and defers the real guard. The mission lists TC-2 as a confirmed defect; shipping zero dedup means a manual LLM re-call of a timed-out start can still double-spawn. Honestly acknowledged and tracked, but Plan 2 actually closes it. Did not flag the MAX_WAIT_SLICE_MS->load-gate interaction that Plan 2 caught (Plan 1 keeps 1000ms slices, sidestepping it).

Co-leader. Matches Plan 1 on every CI anchor (5 name lists, 3 count assertions incl. the checked==36 comment, fixture map, minimal_tool_args, IPC chain with re-exports, parity test) and is MORE complete on TC-2: it adds a narrow daemon-side in-flight fingerprint = hash(argv+cwd+tag) checked at start_combed top, returning the existing job_id within a short TTL -- a REAL dedup guard using std hashing (zero deps), closing the residual manual-retry double-spawn that Plan 1 only defers. This better satisfies the mission's 'TC-2: no idempotency/dedup guard anywhere' as a fixed defect. Sharper CI awareness than Plan 1 in two places: (1) it explicitly flags that lowering MAX_WAIT_SLICE_MS 1000->500 doubles RPC/sec and could regress the TC47 load gate (load_noise_backpressure) -- a real gate Plan 1 didn't consider; (2) it catches that handle_self_check going async forces the alternate dispatch_envelope/named-pipe path to .await too, not just the primary dispatch arm. Deductions: (a) the in-flight fingerprint adds genuine risk -- the TTL entry must be removed on EVERY completion path (exit, cancel, spawn-failure at command.rs:551-560) or it leaks/blocks legit re-runs; Plan 2 flags this but it is net-new daemon mutation in the urgent Phase 1, enlarging the regression-kill phase's blast radius vs Plan 1's pure-classification Phase 1. (b) Phase 1 thus touches command.rs spawn path (cfg-relevant) making both OS gates load-bearing earlier. (c) The MAX_WAIT_SLICE_MS=500 change, while flagged, still INTRODUCES the load-gate risk Plan 1 avoids by leaving slices at 1000ms and using a wall-clock budget instead. Slightly lower scope_discipline/risk than Plan 1 because it does more in the critical early phase; slightly higher completeness offsets it. Net a hair behind Plan 1 for a pure CI-pass-probability lens, ahead on defect-coverage.

Most ambitious, least CI-trustworthy. Adopts ALL of F1 (a)+(b)+(c): no-retry + idempotency key on RequestEnvelope + pre-spawn ack, across 8 phases. Three fatal-for-this-judge problems. (1) ENUMERATION GAP: the phases are one-line stubs ('counts 37->38; fixtures 38/34; docs SPEC s8/s13'); it does NOT name WHICH 5 name lists, WHICH 3 count assertions, or the re-export sites per-phase -- only a generic invariant 'command_stop 5 lists+3 counts+fixtures atomic'. For a ci-gates pass-probability judgment the anchors must be visibly accounted-for; gesturing at them is exactly the half-fix trap research-R3 warns is 'the single biggest easy-to-half-fix trap'. Plans 1/2 enumerate; Plan 3 does not. (2) PRE-SPAWN ACK (P3) is the highest-blast-radius option, which research-gaps explicitly says STILL double-spawns under blind retry unless paired AND rewrites the spawn-failure contract (command.rs:551-560 must flip an already-acked job to Failed) and exposes Starting state at the wire boundary (run_and_watch/command_status liveness mapping). Stacking ack ON TOP of no-retry + idempotency key is triple-redundant: any one of {no-retry + a dedup guard} already kills the auto double-spawn. This inflates risk and surface for marginal value -- the opposite of what keeps each phase independently green. (3) ENVELOPE WIRE CHANGE: P2's idempotency key on client.rs:67-70 RequestEnvelope is a protocol/wire change; Plan 3 never addresses RequestEnvelope back-compat or the additive-serde discipline it would need -- a latent CI/compat hole. Minor errors: P7 lists 'cargo deny' as verification but research-R5 confirms cargo-deny is release-only, NOT in the PR gate; it omits command_stop's sorted-position discipline (the byte-exact sorted asserts need command_stop placed after command_status, before event_context). It DOES correctly catch the windows dispatch_envelope await (P6 'windows:821'). New-IPC-method for CommandWaitNext in P4 (combined status+drain RPC) ALSO triggers the full method_name/DISCOVERABLE_METHODS/dispatch/parity chain -- a second tool-surface-grade change Plan 3 buries in a one-liner, doubling the atomic-anchor risk in a single campaign. Eight phases with two separate IPC-method additions and a wire-envelope change is the least likely to keep every phase independently CI-green.

best_ideas_to_graft: Plan 2's narrow daemon-side in-flight fingerprint dedup guard (hash(argv+cwd+tag), short TTL, returns the existing job_id) grafted onto Plan 1's Phase 1: it CLOSES TC-2 with a real guard using std hashing (zero deps) instead of deferring it, while keeping Plan 1's superior anchor enumeration. This is the single best cross-pollination -- it turns Plan 1's one genuine gap (TC-2 left without an actual dedup guard) into a fixed defect. CRITICAL caveat to carry over: the TTL entry MUST be removed on every completion path (exit, cancel, AND the spawn-failure path at command.rs:551-560) or it leaks/blocks legitimate re-runs.

Plan 2's explicit flag that lowering MAX_WAIT_SLICE_MS doubles RPC/sec and risks regressing the TC47 load gate (load_noise_backpressure) -- the only plan to connect the TC-6 slice fix to a specific CI gate. Better still, adopt Plan 1's wall-clock-deadline-with-min(slice,remaining) approach that achieves the honest cap WITHOUT changing the slice size, sidestepping the load-gate risk entirely.

Plan 2's (and Plan 3's) catch that handle_self_check going async forces the alternate dispatch_envelope / Windows named-pipe dispatch path to .await as well, not just the primary dispatch arm at server.rs:540-543. Plan 1's Phase 4 only mentions the primary arm.

Plan 1's per-phase enumeration discipline (naming the exact 5 name lists, 3 count assertions with line numbers, fixture-map count deltas 37->38 / 33->34, minimal_tool_args reuse-vs-new-arm decision, and the IPC re-export sites) -- this should be the template for ALL phases of whichever plan ships, because it is what makes CI-pass-probability auditable.

Plan 1's honest framing that F1=(a) classify-and-don't-retry is the smallest correct AUTOMATIC double-spawn kill, kept as the urgent Phase 1, with heavier mechanisms (idempotency key, ack) tracked in RISK_REGISTER rather than stacked -- correct posture; combine with Plan 2's lightweight dedup guard rather than Plan 3's full ack handshake.
fatal_flaws: Plan 3 (root-cause-maximalist) does NOT enumerate the per-phase CI anchors for the command_stop tool add or for its TWO new IPC methods (CommandStop in P7 + CommandWaitNext in P4) -- it only states a generic invariant. Research-R3 names the unenumerated atomic-anchor set (5 name lists + 3 count assertions + fixture map + system_discover fixture + minimal_tool_args + IPC method chain + re-exports + parity test) as the single biggest half-fix trap. A plan that gestures at the anchors instead of naming them per-phase cannot be trusted to ship each phase CI-green. This is the decisive flaw for a ci-gates judge.

Plan 3's pre-spawn ack (P3) is triple-redundant with its own P1 (no-retry) + P2 (idempotency key): research-gaps states ack STILL double-spawns under blind retry unless paired, so once no-retry OR a dedup guard exists, the ack buys nothing on the double-spawn while ADDING the highest-blast-radius change in the campaign (rewrites the spawn-failure contract at command.rs:551-560 and exposes Starting state at the wire boundary). Stacking three overlapping mechanisms is the opposite of keeping phases independently green.

Plan 3's P2 puts an idempotency key on the RequestEnvelope (a protocol/wire change) without addressing envelope backward-compat or the additive-serde discipline the repo requires for wire changes -- a latent CI/compat hole; and P4 adds a SECOND new IPC method (CommandWaitNext) that itself triggers the full method_name/DISCOVERABLE_METHODS/dispatch/all_request_variants/parity chain, doubling the new-IPC-surface risk in one campaign.

Not strictly fatal but scope-relevant for ALL plans incl. the leaders: F1=(a) alone (Plan 1) does NOT satisfy the mission's literal 'TC-2: no idempotency/dedup guard anywhere' as a fixed defect -- it removes the AUTOMATIC retry path but ships zero actual guard, so a manual LLM re-call of a timed-out mutating start can still double-spawn. Plan 1 defers the guard (tracked); only Plan 2 actually closes it. Whichever plan ships should adopt Plan 2's lightweight dedup guard to genuinely fix TC-2 rather than reframe it.

Plan 3's P7 lists 'cargo deny' as a verification step, but research-R5 confirms cargo-deny is NOT in the PR gate (release-only) -- a factual CI-machinery error indicating the plan's gate model is approximate. Combined with omitting command_stop's byte-exact sorted-position requirement (after command_status, before event_context, in the sorted asserts at tools.rs:3302 / mcp_stdio / mcp_live_daemon:100), this signals the enumeration is not grounded in the actual assertions.

scores.critique: Strongest plan for an operator. Posture exactly matches the highest-leverage finding from research-gaps: the cheapest correct kill for the TC-1a double-spawn REGRESSION is F1=(a) classify-and-don't-retry mutating RPCs, deferring the heavy idempotency-key protocol (b) and pre-spawn ack (c) with tracked BACKLOG/RISK_REGISTER rows. I verified daemon_client.rs:187-202 has `request` in scope, so `request.is_idempotent()` gating is mechanically valid. It correctly recognizes (and is the only draft that spells out) that into_mcp_error (tools.rs:1667-1726) short-circuits transport errors to transport_unavailable_error() with NO request in scope, so honest mutating remedy text needs the flag threaded from the call site (tools.rs:642 / command_start_combed) -- a real, scoped Phase-1 sub-task, not hand-waved. Phase 1 alone stops the production harm and ships independently: maximal harm-reduction-per-PR. TC-1b+TC-6 co-designed in ONE wait-loop rewrite (Phase 2) using a wall-clock deadline (tokio::time in-tree, zero new RPC, zero new dep) -- the right call vs a new IPC method. TC-3 isolated LAST as its own phase so all 5 name-lists/3 count-assertions/fixture/system_discover churn and the release-please '29-tool' reconciliation land in one reviewable blast, never blocking the urgent trust fixes. Phase 0 establishes the paper trail first. Honest about residual risk: with the blind retry gone, the only remaining double-spawn is a human/LLM manual re-call, which the honest remedy text + TC-1b recovery + TC-4 named rows let the agent avoid. Operator wins: least release surprise, every PR reviewable, no gold-plating, honest artifacts. Minor deductions: phase count for TC-4 (Phase 2 carries run_and_watch tag, Phase 3 carries the wire/render -- a defensible split but a reviewer could see the run_and_watch tag work straddling two phases as slightly awkward); and deferring the daemon-side dedup entirely means a fast human double-tap within the spawn window is still possible (acknowledged and tracked, but trust-contract's narrow in-flight fingerprint closes that hole more cheaply than the deferred full protocol). Completeness vs CI gates is excellent: ZERO new deps, mcp/src guard-safe, both OS gates per phase, integration-through-daemon-IPC test for command_stop, source-status discipline, atomic anchor updates all explicitly enumerated.

Very close second; near-identical correct skeleton to minimal-risk (F1=a primary, wall-clock TC-6, command_stop last, same atomic-anchor and gate awareness, same TC-4/TC-5 forks). The articulated trust axiom ('no error hides a side effect; never tell an agent -- or the adapter on its behalf -- to retry a non-idempotent op; every returned state maps to intent') is the sharpest framing of the three and directly motivates fixing the verified 'retry the tool' remedy text. The differentiator is the daemon-side in-flight fingerprint dedup = hash(argv+cwd+tag) with a short TTL added IN PHASE 1 as defense-in-depth. As an operator I weigh this two ways: it genuinely closes the residual manual-double-tap window that minimal-risk leaves open, uses std hashing (no new dep), and touches no protocol/wire surface -- defensible. BUT it expands Phase 1 (the URGENT regression-kill PR) from a 4-file pure-logic change into a 5-file change that adds stateful daemon behavior (a TTL map that MUST be evicted on every completion path -- exit, cancel, AND spawn-failure at command.rs:551-560, which the plan flags but is a real correctness footgun: a leaked/blocked fingerprint silently blocks a legitimate re-run). That couples a behavioral daemon change to the one PR you most want trivially reviewable and instantly mergeable. The honest minimal move is minimal-risk's: ship the pure retry-gate first, track the dedup. Risk also dings: the fingerprint window/TTL is a heuristic that can collapse two legitimately-distinct rapid identical runs (R2 raised exactly this), and it changes start_combed's hot path. Everything else (TC-1b degraded superset payload with cursor, TC-6 wall-deadline + MAX_WAIT_SLICE_MS 1000->500, TC-3 cancel_tx-clone, TC-5 async cached-bucket profile-gated, TC-4 bounded redacted argv + into_parts fix + render.rs) is correct and complete against the CI gates. Strong plan; loses to minimal-risk purely on Phase-1 scope discipline.

Correct diagnosis, over-built remedy -- the opposite of what an operator wants here. It adopts F1 as (a)+(b)+(c) ALL THREE: P1 stop-retry, P2 server idempotency key on the envelope (threaded through client.rs + server + a daemon TTL map), AND P3 a full pre-spawn ack handshake (Starting state, spawn-fail->Failed+synthetic event, command.rs:547/711). This directly contradicts research-gaps' headline guidance that (a) is the smallest correct fix the others under-weighted, and it self-contradicts: its own explicitly_deferred list is short while it BUILDS the very (b)/(c) machinery the other two correctly defer. Once the P1 retry-gate lands, the AUTOMATIC double-spawn is already dead -- so P2 and P3 are belt-AND-suspenders-AND-a-second-belt for a harm already removed, each carrying real blast radius (pre-spawn ack rewrites the spawn-failure contract and exposes a wire-level Starting liveness that run_and_watch/command_status must learn -- R2 flagged this as the largest-blast-radius option). Worse for TC-6: P4 introduces a NEW CommandWaitNext IPC method (4 sync surfaces + parity test at server.rs:1045) purely to fix a wall-clock cap that a localized arithmetic deadline fixes with zero protocol surface -- and it COLLIDES with TC-3's IPC-method work, exactly the conflict the other drafts avoid. Net: 7 phases (the most), the most code touched, the most release-please churn and version-bump exposure, and the most surfaces that must move atomically -- every extra IPC method is another half-fix-CI-red trap. The terse format also drops operator-critical detail: P1 lists 'fix remedy tools.rs:1797' as a bare bullet with no recognition that into_mcp_error has no request in scope (the verified -32603 'retry the tool' issue), so the honest-remedy fix is under-specified vs the other two. Fatal for this judge: it maximizes production-correctness theory at the cost of reviewability, release safety, and scope -- it gold-plates the single defect that the cheap fix already neutralizes. The phase ordering (docs first, command_stop last, blast-radius order) and the invariants (zero deps, mcp/src guard-safe, both OS gates, integration tests, add-only RISK_REGISTER) are all correct, which keeps correctness/completeness respectable, but scope and risk are where an operator lives and this draft loses both.

best_ideas_to_graft: From trust-contract-first: the narrow daemon-side in-flight fingerprint = hash(argv+cwd+tag) with a short TTL (std hashing, no protocol/wire change, no new dep) is a cheap defense-in-depth that closes the residual MANUAL double-tap window minimal-risk leaves open. Graft it -- but as a SEPARATE small phase AFTER the urgent retry-gate PR (do NOT load it into Phase 1), with mandatory eviction on every completion path (exit, cancel, AND spawn-failure at command.rs:551-560) and a test proving a leaked fingerprint never blocks a legitimate re-run.

From trust-contract-first: the explicit design axiom ('no returned error hides a side effect; never instruct retry of a non-idempotent op -- including the ADAPTER retrying on the agent's behalf; every returned state maps to caller intent'). Adopt this as the campaign's stated invariant -- it is the cleanest justification for both the retry-gate AND the honest remedy-text fix, and it gives reviewers a one-line test for every error path touched.

From trust-contract-first/minimal-risk both: TC-1b degraded payload as a strict SUPERSET of the existing run_and_watch result (reuse complete/wait_exhausted, add only degraded:true + recover_hint, plus surface the already-internal cursor so the caller can resume the subscription). Superset = no existing assertion or reader breaks. Keep minimal-risk's rule that the START-arm error (no job_id yet) still returns Err.

From trust-contract-first: lowering MAX_WAIT_SLICE_MS 1000->500 in the TC-6 fix halves worst-case per-slice overshoot -- a cheap addition to the wall-clock-deadline rewrite. Graft it but explicitly re-run the load_noise_backpressure (TC47) gate, since it doubles RPC frequency.

From minimal-risk (the load-bearing structural insight): thread the mutating flag from the CALL SITE into the remedy text, because into_mcp_error (verified tools.rs:1678-1680) short-circuits transport errors with NO request in scope -- so the mutating-aware 'the command MAY have started; call command_status/runtime_state before re-running' message cannot be produced inside into_mcp_error alone. Any plan that says 'fix remedy text' without this is under-specified.
fatal_flaws: root-cause-maximalist: builds F1 (a)+(b)+(c) -- it implements the server idempotency key (P2) AND the pre-spawn ack handshake (P3) that the research and the other two drafts correctly DEFER, after P1's retry-gate has already eliminated the automatic double-spawn. This is gold-plating the exact harm the cheap fix removes, contradicting research-gaps' explicit 'under-weighted (a)' guidance and its own (short) deferral list. Maximal code, maximal release churn, minimal marginal harm-reduction -- disqualifying for an operator judge.

root-cause-maximalist: P4 introduces a NEW CommandWaitNext IPC method solely to fix the TC-6 wall-time cap, when a localized wall-clock-deadline arithmetic fix achieves the same advertised-cap guarantee with ZERO protocol surface. The new method adds another set of atomic sync surfaces (method_name/DISCOVERABLE_METHODS/dispatch/parity test) and COLLIDES with the TC-3 command_stop IPC-method work -- two new IPC methods in one campaign is exactly the half-fix-CI-red multiplier the constraints warn against.

root-cause-maximalist: 7 phases including two separate stateful daemon-protocol phases (idempotency key, pre-spawn ack) maximize release-please version-bump exposure and reviewer load while the urgent regression is already fixed by phase 1 -- worst reviewability/release-safety profile of the three.

trust-contract-first (non-fatal but the deciding deduction): folds the daemon in-flight fingerprint dedup (a stateful behavioral daemon change with a TTL eviction footgun across exit/cancel/spawn-failure paths) INTO Phase 1, the one PR you most want to be trivially reviewable and instantly mergeable to stop the production double-spawn. Coupling new daemon state to the urgent regression-kill is a scope-discipline miss; minimal-risk's pure-logic Phase 1 is the safer operator move.

None of the three is invalid; all respect the hard constraints (zero deps, mcp/src spawn/fs/socket-free with kill forwarded over IPC, both OS gates, integration-through-daemon-IPC test for command_stop, atomic anchor updates, add-only RISK_REGISTER, .planning open-work shape, no push/merge without approval, live daemon untouched via own sockets). The separation is purely scope/sequencing/release-safety, where minimal-risk > trust-contract-first >> root-cause-maximalist.
